subject: Project Management
title: The Segway
description: A quick, practical guide to The Segway.

hook.text: Before launch, the Segway was called 'bigger than the internet.' Steve Jobs invested early.

buildup.text: Inventor Dean Kamen spent $100 million developing a self-balancing personal transporter.

discovery.text: The technology was brilliant — gyroscopes kept riders upright using the same physics as spacecraft.

twist.text: It cost $5,000, was illegal on sidewalks in many cities, and people felt ridiculous riding it.

climax.text: Kamen predicted 10,000 units per week. Segway sold 30,000 in its first two years — total.

punchline.text: A brilliant invention that solved a problem nobody had.

quiz.question: Why did the Segway fail to meet expectations?
options: It was expensive, legally restricted, and socially awkward to ride
It was too fast for public use
